what_it_does: Acceptance test for the right-hand-man (RHM) chat substrate: it verifies that the chat log is append-only, oldest-first, bounded, and persists across FsStore sessions, and that the chat context builder carries compact live system state (graph name, node count, available node types) without dumping the codebase.
observations: Uses a custom check(label, cond) helper that increments a global PASS counter and raises AssertionError on failure.
Creates a temporary FsStore, writes chat turns, and checks chat_history ordering, role retention, and limit behavior.
Reopens the same store path with a second FsStore instance to assert chat persistence across sessions.
Discovers nodes, creates a small graph with constant and uppercase nodes, and calls suite._chat_context(g).
Asserts the context contains the graph name, the live node count, and available node types.
Asserts the context does not contain AGENTS.md and is under a 16000-character budget.
Docstring and comments reference COMPACT GROUND TRUTH, continuity, context-05 escalation rung 1, and a budget raise from 4000 to 16000 dated 2026-06-22.
The script cleans up the temporary directory in a finally block.
standouts: Explicit no-codebase-dump invariant enforced by both content check (AGENTS.md absent) and a hard context-length cap.
The test calls a private method suite._chat_context, coupling the acceptance test to internal implementation.
A dated comment records a budget decision by a named person (Tim 2026-06-22).
conventions: Follows a lightweight inline test harness rather than pytest/unittest.
Uses sys.path insertion to locate the project root.
Uses tempfile for an isolated, self-cleaning store directory.
Docstring states the file's purpose and scope before the code.
concerns: Tests depend on the private method _chat_context, making them fragile to internal refactoring.
The 16000-character context cap is arbitrary and may break with legitimate growth of compact ground truth.
The global PASS counter is not thread-safe and mixes test state with the module namespace.
The docstring admits the actual model reply and browser behavior are not tested here, so the file only partially proves the stated acceptance goal.
A comment references a future/stale date (2026-06-22) that may confuse readers.
notes: A substrate-level acceptance test for the RHM chat that checks persistent chat history and a bounded, live-state context builder.
questions: What is 'context-05 escalation rung 1' and where is it defined?
What is the '88k codebase' and why is AGENTS.md specifically named as the forbidden dump?
What does the uppercase node do, and why was it chosen for this context test?
What is a 'raw-ui://' dump and how is it capped to Chrome regions?
How is the actual model reply proven in the browser for ground-truth-dependent questions?
Why was the context budget set to 16000 bytes/characters specifically?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to prove the RHM chat substrate (grounded context + persistent chat log). The file actually tests the store's chat history and the suite's context builder in isolation; it does not exercise the real model or browser surface, so it matches partially.
apparent_themes: Compact ground truth over full codebase dumps
Session continuity and persistent conversation memory
Grounding chat responses in live system state
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that the RHM chat is grounded in live, compact system state rather than inferred from source code, and that its conversation memory survives across sessions.
novelty: It encodes an explicit anti-pattern guard — no codebase dumps — with both a content probe (AGENTS.md) and a length cap, and it documents a budget decision with a named author and date.
contribution: Provides concrete behavioral assertions for chat-log semantics (ordering, limits, persistence) and context-builder boundaries (live node facts only, no source dumps, length cap) that a fused system would keep as contracts for the RHM panel.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying the RHM chat log is append-only, oldest-first, bounded, persistent across sessions, and that the chat context builder includes only compact live system state without codebase dumps.
intention_for_embedding: Prove the RHM conversational substrate maintains persistent, chronologically ordered chat history and a bounded, live-state context for grounded replies.

symbols.does: Lightweight assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and raises an AssertionError on failure.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition; if true, it increments the module-level PASS counter and prints an 'ok' message with the label, otherwise it raises AssertionError prefixed with 'FAIL: {label}'. It serves as the script's minimal test harness.
